section.heading: Art. 104. Public records offenses
section.chapeau: Any person subject to this chapter who, willfully and unlawfully—

paragraph.content: alters, conceals, removes, mutilates, obliterates, or destroys a public record; or
takes a public record with the intent to alter, conceal, remove, mutilate, obliterate, or destroy the public record;

section.continuation: shall be punished as a court-martial may direct.
section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 114–328, div. E, title LX, § 5415, Dec. 23, 2016, 130 Stat. 2944.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 904 was renumbered section 903b of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective on Jan. 1, 2019, as designated by the President, with implementing regulations and provisions relating to applicability to various situations, see section 5542 of Pub. L. 114–328 and Ex. Ord. No. 13825, set out as notes under section 801 of this title.
